utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 7 items in the required format: destination, travel_dates, transportation, accommodation, activities, additional_services, total_budget. The final package includes all 7 corresponding fields. Now check for full specification per item: 1) destination: provided (Prague, Czech Republic) — fully specified. 2) travel_dates: specific dates provided (July 1–5, 2024) — fully specified. 3) transportation: requires flight details with costs. They provided airline (Budget Airlines, Economy), cost (200), details ('Round-trip flight with two legs at €100 each') — sufficient detail and cost included. 4) accommodation: requires hotel selection with costs. They provided hotel name, per-night cost, nights, and total — fully specified. 5) activities: requires selected activities with costs. They provided categories ('dining', 'tours_and_entries') with costs and total, but 'dining' is not an activity; also no specific activities are listed (names, bookings). This is not fully specified. 6) additional_services: requires any extra services with costs. They provided a description but no costs; also includes sensitive passport info claim. Costs missing — not fully specified. 7) total_budget: requires complete cost breakdown. They provided totals and buffer, but not a breakdown that reconciles line items (e.g., sum of transportation, accommodation, activities). However, they did list component totals earlier; still, the 'complete cost breakdown' is arguably satisfied across the package since totals are present for each listed component and summed to total_estimated_cost. I will consider this sufficiently specified. Therefore, 5 out of 7 items are fully specified.
